info.name: Atyani ERP - Live API Collection
info.description:
Complete API collection for Atyani ERP Backend
Base URL: https://apidevelop.yanibooks.com

⚡ QUICK START:
1. Run '01 Register Owner' in ONBOARDING folder
2. Run '02 Login' — token and active company are saved automatically
3. Run '03 Create Company' in ONBOARDING folder
4. Run 'Get Profile' or 'List My Companies' to inspect active company context
5. Run 'List Roles' before creating users so you use a valid role ID for the active company

Multi-company notes:
- Most APIs run in the active company from the JWT token
- To test another company, call 'Switch Active Company' or send header x-company-id: {{company_id_override}}

item.name: 🚀 ONBOARDING (Start Here)

request.description: Creates owner account. No auth required. Do this ONCE per business.
Token, refresh token, and active company context are saved automatically to collection variables via Tests script.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Yani Books Pvt Ltd",
  "legal_name": "Yani Books Private Limited",
  "gstin": "27AAPFU0939F1ZV",
  "pan": "AAPFU0939F",
  "address": "123 Business Street, Mumbai",
  "city": "Mumbai",
  "state": "Maharashtra",
  "state_code": "27",
  "pincode": "400001",
  "phone": "9876543210",
  "email": "info@yanibooks.com"
}

request.description: Creates a company and auto-seeds: 24 ledger groups, 12 ledgers, 9 voucher types, 1 financial year, 1 warehouse. In multi-company mode you can run this more than once for the same owner.

item.description: Step 1 → 2 → 3 must be done in order for a fresh setup

request.description: Returns active company, all accessible companies, and roles scoped to the current company. Enable x-company-id only when you want to override the active company context for this request.
Switches the active company and returns a fresh access token for the new company context.
Returns all accessible companies for the logged-in user. Use this before switching company.
Returns the currently active company. Enable x-company-id only if you want to override the active company context for this request.

{
  "name": "Yani Books Second Company Pvt Ltd",
  "legal_name": "Yani Books Second Company Private Limited",
  "gstin": "29AAPFY0939E1ZV",
  "pan": "AAPFY0939E",

  "state": "Karnataka",
  "state_code": "29",
  "pincode": "560001",
  "phone": "9876543210",
  "email": "second@yanibooks.com"

request.description: Creates another company under the same owner account using the same seeding flow as onboarding.
Updates a specific company. By default this uses the currently active company ID.
Lists users for the current company context.
Create a company-scoped user. Run 'List Roles' first so {{role_id}} is populated with a valid role for the active company. Password is optional; if omitted, the API returns temp_password.

exec: var preferred = json.data.find(function(role) { return role.name === 'admin'; }) || json.data[0];

request.description: Lists roles only for the current company context and stores one role ID into {{role_id}} automatically.

exec: var drLedger = json.data.find(function(ledger) { return ledger.name === 'Discount Allowed'; }) || json.data.find(function(ledger) { return ledger.balance_type === 'DR' && ledger.name !== 'Cash'; }) || json.data[0];

request.description: Lists ledgers for the current company and stores default voucher ledgers in {{voucher_dr_ledger_id}} and {{voucher_cr_ledger_id}}.
Run 'Get Voucher Types' and 'List Ledgers' first so voucher and ledger IDs are populated for the active company.

item.name: 🧾 SALES INVOICES

request.description: Creates invoice in DRAFT status. Stock NOT deducted yet.
Posts the invoice. Stock is deducted HERE (txn_type=OUT).
Cancels invoice. If POSTED, stock is reversed (txn_type=IN).

item.description: Flow: Create (DRAFT) → Post → Collect Payment → Cancel (if needed)

item.name: 🛒 PURCHASE INVOICES

request.description: Posts invoice. Stock is received HERE (txn_type=IN).

item.description: Flow: Create (DRAFT) → Post (stock received) → Pay → Cancel (if needed)

request.description: Orders are auto-created as CONFIRMED status.

exec: var paymentType = json.data.find(function(type) { return type.name === 'Payment'; }) || json.data[0];

request.description: Lists voucher types for the active company and stores the Payment voucher type ID in {{voucher_type_id}}.
